description: A male instructor in a sky-blue rash guard teaches a small red-haired baby using a yellow dive disc for engagement, a red-and-white beach ball bouncing in the background. The busy deck details give a welcoming, playful feel.
The same coach cradles the red-haired baby close, baby still gripping the yellow dive disc. Another moment of engaged, gentle instruction.

A male 'COACH' instructor plays with two toddlers — a red-haired baby and a pink-donut-print girl — as a pink-goggled sister watches from the foreground. The group shot shows the school's sibling-friendly vibe.
The same coach supports the small girl in the pink donut rash guard through a back-float, her eyes closed in full trust. A peaceful 'I can float' image.
Top-down close-up of a little girl in pink goggles and a pink ruffled swimsuit, smiling up from the pool as another swimmer blurs in the background. Ideal vertical social image.
The same pink-goggled, pink-suited girl now with a softer smile, hands just above the water. A clean, photogenic 'swim-class portrait' shot.
A teen male instructor in a light-blue 'hard things' rash guard watches a toddler in a cupcake-print swim top practice back-floating alone. The kid's free kicks suggest solo progression.
Same coach and cupcake-suit girl — she kicks her way across the water on her back with minimal support. The visible arc of progression is great for a 'milestone' section.
The coach pulls a small girl in a pink mermaid suit across the pool surface by her hand, demonstrating streamline glide practice. Captures the moment of initial momentum.
A goggled boy in orange goggles swims forward in a breaststroke-like position while his ocean-print instructor watches alongside. Shows older-beginner stroke practice.
Same boy continues the swim drill, goggles flashing, mouth open for breath. A clean action shot for 'bigger kids' swim programming.
A teal 'COACH' instructor smiles at a small blonde girl in a pink donut-print rash guard as they share a moment in the water. The open-mouth baby laugh is adorable content.
Same pair from a slightly different angle — the baby's thumb in mouth as the coach grins. Candid moments like this read as authentic parent-appeal content.
A male ocean-print coach stands beside a small girl in a pink-and-purple swim cap and pink goggles in the pool. Another student in goggles is soft-focused on the right.
Same coach and swim-capped girl — she's deep in thought about her next skill, the coach patiently watching. Shows the 'thinking' side of swim instruction.
The coach smiles at the swim-capped girl as she grips the pool wall, another goggled swimmer blurred in background. Clean instructional moment.
Close frame of the same coach and the pink-goggled swim-capped girl, the girl looking down thoughtfully. Reads as 'quiet reflection between drills.'
Male 'COACH' in teal supports a girl in pink goggles practicing breath control with a green pool noodle just visible at her chin. Shows the progression toward independent skill use.
The 'hard things' coach holds a red-haired toddler gently in the water, the child's bare torso visible. A soft 'coach-and-kid' story image.
A smiling coach and a goggled girl in a pink patterned suit work together on a bright green kickboard drill. The pair's shared joy sells the 'learning is fun here' message.
